regulationSummary.overview: Kansas has no gold deposits and is not a gold prospecting destination. The state is underlain entirely by sedimentary rock formations — Permian salt and gypsum, Cretaceous chalk, and Quaternary alluvium — with no gold-bearing geology. Kansas is an excellent destination for fossil hunting (Niobrara Chalk fossils, mosasaurs, pterosaurs) but offers nothing for gold prospectors.
keyRules: Kansas does not have a recreational gold prospecting permit system — there are no gold deposits in the state.
Fossil collecting on Kansas public land (Cimarron National Grassland) is regulated — vertebrate fossils require a permit; invertebrates and plants can be collected for personal use.
In-stream work in Kansas waterways requires coordination with the Kansas Department of Agriculture and Army Corps of Engineers.
regulationSummary.hpaInfo: Not applicable — there are no gold-bearing streams in Kansas.
regulationSummary.suctionDredgeStatus: Not applicable — there are no productive placer gold streams in Kansas.
regulationSummary.handPanning: Gold panning in Kansas streams is not a viable activity — the state has no gold-bearing geology. Kansas prospectors should instead explore the state's remarkable fossil record on the Cimarron National Grassland.
regulationSummary.disclaimer: Regulations change. Verify current rules at the official sources listed below before going out.

officialResources.title: Kansas Geological Survey
officialResources.description: State geological survey — Kansas has no gold deposits; the survey focuses on oil, gas, water, salt, and chalk geology.

officialResources.title: Cimarron National Grassland
officialResources.description: The only significant USFS-managed public land in Kansas — allows fossil collecting under specific rules but gold prospecting is not viable.
